top_stories.title: Identity security firm SailPoint discloses GitHub repository breach
top_stories.summary: SailPoint disclosed a breach of its GitHub repository on April 20, stating the incident has been contained. The company reported that no customer data was affected by the breach.

top_stories.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
top_stories.summary: Google disclosed that a threat actor used an AI-developed zero-day exploit to bypass two-factor authentication (2FA), marking the first known instance of AI being used in the wild for malicious vulnerability discovery and exploit generation. The attack was attributed to cybercrime threat actors and represents a novel threat vector directly targeting multi-factor authentication mechanisms.

top_stories.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
top_stories.summary: Fortinet released security patches addressing two critical vulnerabilities in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could allow attackers to execute arbitrary commands or code. FortiAuthenticator is Fortinet's authentication and identity management product, making this directly relevant to IAM security.

top_stories.title: The hidden risk of non-human identities in AI adoption
top_stories.summary: The article examines security risks associated with non-human identities (NHIs) in the context of AI adoption, focusing on how AI agents and service accounts are often granted persistent, broadly privileged access without proper governance controls. Key concerns include the lack of audit trails, undefined ownership, and absence of regular access reviews for NHIs including AI agents, API keys, and traditional service accounts.

top_stories.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
top_stories.summary: Researchers have disclosed a new Linux backdoor called PamDOORa, sold on a Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600, that abuses Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) to steal SSH credentials. The toolkit enables persistent SSH access via a hardcoded magic password and specific TCP port combination, functioning as a post-exploitation tool targeting Linux authentication infrastructure.

news.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
news.summary: Hundreds of npm packages in the TanStack open source ecosystem have been infected by a self-propagating, credential-stealing worm attributed to TeamPCP. The worm spreads through supply chain compromise and steals credentials from affected systems.

news.title: Stealthy hackers exploit cPanel flaw in active backdoor campaign (CVE-2026-41940)
news.summary: Security researchers at XLab have identified an active attack campaign exploiting CVE-2026-41940, a vulnerability in cPanel & WHM that allows attackers to log in without a username or password, granting full administrator control. The flaw effectively bypasses all authentication on affected cPanel servers, compromising hosted websites, configurations, and databases.

news.title: How to use streamlined permissions for Amazon S3 Tables and Iceberg materialized views
news.summary: AWS published a guide on configuring access controls for Amazon S3 Tables and Iceberg materialized views using IAM-based authorization in the AWS Glue Data Catalog. The article focuses primarily on analytics infrastructure setup, with IAM used as a supporting mechanism for permissions management rather than as the central subject.

news.title: VU#937808: Casdoor contains Arbitrary File Write vulnerability
news.summary: Casdoor, an open-source identity and access management platform, contains an arbitrary file write vulnerability (VU#937808) in its Local File System storage provider. An authenticated user with file upload permissions can escape the intended upload directory due to insufficient sanitization of user-supplied paths.

news.title: Attackers targeting storage infrastructure for remote work
news.summary: Threat actors are increasingly targeting storage infrastructure by exploiting identity gaps, cloud misconfigurations, and zero trust weaknesses to cripple backups and deploy ransomware. The article frames remote work environments and storage systems as attack surfaces enabled by identity and zero trust deficiencies.

news.title: SEON Appoints Sagnik Nandy, CTO of Docusign, to Board of Directors
news.summary: SEON, a fraud prevention company, has appointed Sagnik Nandy to its board of directors. Nandy previously served as CTO of Docusign and held engineering leadership roles at Google and Okta.

news.title: Europe wants out from under US tech – but first it has to find the exits
news.summary: A report has mapped European dependencies on US technology, identifying weaknesses in cloud infrastructure, identity systems, and public sector procurement. The analysis highlights identity as one of the areas where European institutions face significant reliance on US-based vendors.

news.title: Hackers Trick DigiCert Into Issuing Certificates Used to Sign Malware
news.summary: DigiCert revoked 60 code signing certificates after hackers tricked the company's support chat into issuing certificates that were used to sign Zhong Stealer malware. The attack involved a malicious attachment submitted through DigiCert's support chat portal, resulting in fraudulently obtained certificates being used to sign malware.

news.title: A new frontier: Identity stack evolves for agentic systems
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how the identity stack is evolving to support agentic AI systems. The article focuses on identity becoming a real-time control plane as AI agents require new approaches to authentication and authorization.

news.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed PCPJack, a credential theft framework that targets cloud infrastructure by exploiting five CVEs to spread worm-like across systems. The toolset harvests credentials from cloud, container, developer, productivity, and financial services before exfiltrating data through attacker-controlled infrastructure.

news.title: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
news.summary: Microsoft published a blog post for World Passkey Day highlighting its efforts to advance passkey adoption as a passwordless authentication method. The post covers Microsoft's initiatives to replace passwords, reduce phishing risk, and improve sign-in security through passkey technology.

news.title: 5 leading enterprise password managers to consider
news.summary: The article outlines key features enterprise password managers should provide for administrators. It covers available options and capabilities relevant to credential and access management in enterprise environments.

news.title: Semperis Names John Podboy Chief Information Security Officer
news.summary: Semperis has appointed John Podboy as its new Chief Information Security Officer. Podboy will focus on risk, resilience, compliance, and agentic AI-driven cyber defense at the identity-focused cybersecurity company.

news.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
news.summary: Attackers are abusing Windows Phone Link using CloudZ RAT and a new plugin called Pheno to hijack the PC-smartphone bridge and intercept SMS messages. The attack enables bypassing of SMS-based two-factor authentication by stealing text messages routed through the Windows Phone Link application.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing an attacker with administrative privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability raises concerns for enterprise environments where credential theft could enable lateral movement or privilege escalation.

news.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
news.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits emerging shortly after. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation activity has been occurring for at least a month prior to disclosure, putting millions of cPanel-managed systems at risk.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in phishing attacks against major technology companies in 2022. The attacks involved SMS phishing campaigns that compromised at least a dozen companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
